body.title: Archivist: propose proven local patterns to global KB
body.result: devices/archivist/contrib.py: scan/list/submit CLI; CONTRIB_CANDIDATE channel post; context stripping; 44 tests pass
body.description:
The Archivist's contribution loop: locally-discovered patterns that have proven themselves (hit rate above threshold, N uses, generality score) get staged as proposed contributions to the global KB git repo. Human reviews and submits the PR — the Archivist proposes, humans gate community contributions.

Flow: Archivist identifies candidate pattern → strips instance-specific context → writes to contribution staging area → posts CONTRIB_CANDIDATE to shared channel with summary → human runs `uu contrib submit` to open the PR.

**Affected files:** devices/archivist/contrib.py (new); `uu` CLI (contrib subcommand); contrib staging area (local path gitignored from UU repo)

**Design rules:** human-in-loop for all global KB contributions; no auto-PR; strip PII and instance context before staging

**Scope boundary:** IN — candidate detection (threshold-based); context stripping; staging area; channel notification; `uu contrib submit` CLI. OUT — auto-merge; governance tooling; pattern quality scoring (uses simple hit-count threshold)

**Completion criteria:** (1) Archivist posts CONTRIB_CANDIDATE to channel when a pattern crosses threshold; (2) `uu contrib list` shows staged candidates; (3) `uu contrib submit <id>` opens a PR draft (or prints the diff for manual PR); (4) staged files contain no instance paths, ticket IDs, or credentials
